status: Public-safe agent and review interface source
title: Agent Review Neural Interface
subtitle: A source-backed view of a governed agent operating layer: source systems, agent roles, review gates, risk signals, and handoff flow.

publicSafety.scope: Layer 3 source systems and agent/review architecture only.
publicSafety.artifactStatus: Static public-facing review artifact; not a live dashboard or control system.

sourceSystems.title: Role Registry
sourceSystems.description: Maintains which agents exist, whether they are usable, and which contract backs each role.

sourceSystems.title: Stage Contracts
sourceSystems.description: Defines how work enters, gets judged, produces output, and hands off to the next owner.

sourceSystems.title: Build-Time Review Standard
sourceSystems.description: Routes substantial build work through architecture, evidence, boundary, model, risk, and signal checks before execution.

sourceSystems.title: Risk Standard
sourceSystems.description: Scores work before movement and names the failure mode, review gate, and stop condition.

sourceSystems.title: Signal Standard
sourceSystems.description: Packages state so work can move between agents, stages, and sessions without losing context.

sourceSystems.title: Boundary Rules
sourceSystems.description: Blocks unsafe movement around live systems, credentials, permissions, and external commitments.

sourceSystems.title: Memory And Handoff Logs
sourceSystems.description: Preserves decisions, run records, source updates, and next-owner packets across sessions.

sourceSystems.title: Generator Contract
sourceSystems.description: Keeps public artifacts subordinate to source maps and prevents the visual surface from becoming the source of truth.

agentGroups.title: Core Operating Agents
agentGroups.description: The agents that hold routing, proof, memory, signal, boundary, and expression responsibilities.

agents.title: Truth Gate Agent
agents.description: Checks whether claims are supported, uncertain, or blocked before movement.

agents.title: Routing Agent
agents.description: Determines destination, sequence, owner, review gate, and handoff path.

agents.title: Memory Judgment Agent
agents.description: Decides what prior context matters and what must persist after the run.

agents.title: Record Agent
agents.description: Keeps exact record, provenance, timestamps, and source traceability.

agents.title: Index Agent
agents.description: Makes sources, decisions, outputs, and handoffs findable later.

agents.title: Risk Agent
agents.description: Scores risk and names the main failure mode before work moves.

agents.title: Signal Agent
agents.description: Packages routing, risk, state, audience, and next action into a portable packet.

agents.title: Boundary Agent
agents.description: Stops unsafe movement around live systems, credentials, and permissions.

agents.title: Voice Agent
agents.description: Adapts expression only after source, proof, and audience are clear.

agentGroups.title: Review And Specialist Agents
agentGroups.description: The review layer that checks evidence, boundaries, model quality, build readiness, repair needs, protocol compliance, intake context, and field reality.

agents.title: Evidence Agent
agents.description: Separates what is known, assumed, missing, and source-backed.

agents.title: Boundary Architecture Agent
agents.description: Defines safe movement envelopes and what must not be crossed.

agents.title: Model Coherence Agent
agents.description: Checks whether the underlying abstraction is coherent, practical, and not overfit.

agents.title: Build Agent
agents.description: Executes implementation only after routing and review authorize movement.

agents.title: Repair Agent
agents.description: Diagnoses breakdowns, repeated failures, and process corrections.

agents.title: Protocol Agent
agents.description: Checks deterministic rules, acceptance tests, and rules-as-written compliance.

agents.title: Intake Signal Agent
agents.description: Captures human context, request shape, and close-to-ground signal.

agents.title: Field Context Agent
agents.description: Checks terrain, environmental reality, and practical operating context.

stageSpine.title: 01 Intake
stageSpine.description: Receive work, load context, define scope, and identify source requirements.

stageSpine.title: 02 Judgment
stageSpine.description: Apply role authority, constraints, checks, and failure conditions.

stageSpine.title: 03 Output
stageSpine.description: Produce the agent-specific packet, review finding, artifact, or route.

stageSpine.title: 04 Handoff
stageSpine.description: Move work to the next owner, stage, memory destination, or approval gate.

reviewGates.title: Evidence Gate
reviewGates.description: Blocks confident movement when source support is missing.

reviewGates.title: Boundary Gate
reviewGates.description: Blocks movement that would imply live access, credentials, or external commitments.

reviewGates.title: Model Gate
reviewGates.description: Challenges elegant but impractical abstractions before they become system law.

reviewGates.title: Risk Gate
reviewGates.description: Names the score, failure mode, review gate, and stop condition.

reviewGates.title: Signal Gate
reviewGates.description: Makes the current state portable enough for the next role or session.

reviewGates.title: Build Authorization Gate
reviewGates.description: Only allows implementation after routing and review permit movement.

publicNotes: This is a static architecture showcase, not a live dashboard.
Agent cards describe contracted roles and review responsibilities, not autonomous production bots.
No client lanes, private operating status, credentials, or live-system access are shown.
The visual layer is generated from a source map so the artifact can be refreshed without hand-editing the HTML.
